Fnrn Polrrrcel PRAcrrcps CoMMrssroN
-128 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95gl.l-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
April 8.2008
David L. Morton
Cor-rnty of San Diego / Registrar of Voters
Financial Disclosure Desk
P.O. Box 85656
San Diego, CA. 92186-5656
Re
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-08-036
Dear Mr. Morton:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarcling the conflict-of-interest
code provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act";.r Because your request is general
itt nature and does not involve a specific proceeding before the agency, we'must treat
yollr request as one frrr informal assistance.r
QUESTTON
Under Section 87302.3(a), must every candidate for an elective office file a
statement of economic interests ("SEI") if the office is a designated position in an
agency's conflict-of-interest code or must the code specificaliy designate candidates for
the office?
CONCLUSION
Section 87302.3(a) requires every candidate for an elective office to file a SEI if
the office is a designated position in an agency's conflict-of-interest code. This
requirement applies even when the agency's code has not specifically designated
candidates for the elective office.
I The Political Retorm Act is contained in Government Code Sections g 1000 through g l0l.1. All
statutory ret'erences are to the Government Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulationi of the Fair
Political Practices commission are contained in Sections l8ll0 through lgggT of Title 2 of the calitbrnia
Code of Regulations. All regulatory ret'erences are to Title 2. Division-6 of the California Code of
Regulations. unless otherwise indieated.
I Please n()te that intbrmal assistance {t1s n9t provicle the requesbr with immunitl, provided by
an opinion or tbrmal wrirten advice. (Seetion tt3 lr.l: Reguration tg:zsrcrt3t. encrosed.)


File No. I-08-036
Page No. 2
FACTS
As a representative of the County of San Diego's Registrar of Voters, you are
requesting advice regarding Section 87302.3(a) of the Act, which was added to the Act
by Senate Bill 512 in20o7.
ANALYSIS
Section 87302.3(a) of the Act provides:
"Every candidate for an elective office that is designated in a
conflict of interest code shall file a statement disclosing his or her
investments, business positions, interests in real property, and income
received during the immediately preceding 12 months, as enumerated in
the disclosure requirements for that position. The statement shall be filed
with the election official with whom the candidate's declaration of
candidacy or other nomination documents to appear on the ballot are
required to be filed and shall be filed no latter than the final filing date for
the declaration or nomination documents."
As you have pointed out, Section 87302.3(a) does not clearly specify whether
candidates for an elective office must file a SEI if the position is designated in an
agency's conflict-of-interest code or whether candidates are only required to file a SEI if
the agency's code specifically designates candidates for the office. Accordingly, we must
turn to the rules of statutory construction to determine the scope of the Section 
-
87302.3(a). As described in the Vila Advice Letter, No. I-99-006:
"'The goal of statutory construction is to ascertain and effectuate
the intent of the Legislature .' (Pacffic Gas & Electric Co. v. Countlt of
stanislaus (1997) 16 cal.4th 1143, 1152.) Generaly, the language of a
statute is the most reliable indication of legislative intent. (Calvillo- Silva
v. Home Grocery (1998) 19 Cal.4th 714,724.) However, when the
language of the statute is ambiguous, a court will consider both the
legislative history of the statute and the wider historical circumstances of
its enactment to determine the legislative intent. (watts v. crawford
(1995) 10 Cal.4th 743,753.)"
Looking to the Senate Floor Analysis of Section 57302.3(a), the Office of Senate
Floor Analyses provided the following:
"[s]tate and local public officials are required to file an SEI if the
position they hold is designated in an agency's conflict of interest code. A
position becomes "designated" when the position entails the making or
participation in the making of governmental decisions that may have a


.
:lj
i
File No. I-08-036
Page No. 3
foreseeable material financial effect on the decision maker,s financial
interests. [...] However, there is nothing in the [Act] requiring candidates
for these offices to file an SEI prior to their election. Thii depiives the
public of important information about personal financial interests that may
affect a candidate's ability to perform hi./h". duties if elected to office.
This bill closes that loophole by requiring candidates for these positions
designated in an agency's conflict of interest code to file SEIs in the same
manner as other candidates." (Senate Rules Committee Office of Senate
Floor Analyses, Senate Floor Analysis of Senate Bill 512, August 29,
2007.)
While still not.clearlyindicating whether Section 87302.3(a) requires a candidate
for elective office to file a SEI if an agincy's conflict-of-interest code does not
specifically designate candidates for the oifi"", the floor analysis does reveal the
Legislature's concern if information regarding candidates' financial interests is deprived
from the public. In light of this .on.".n, the irterpretation of section g7302.3(a) most
consistent with the purposes of the Act is to requiie all candidates for an elective office to
file an SEI if the office is designated in an agency's conflict-of-interest code even if the
code does not specifically designate candidaies.
Moreover, this,conclusion is supported by the historical circumstances of the
enactment of Section 87302.3(a). As stated in the McNctmaraAdvice Letter, No. I-93-
487, "The inclusion of candidates in a conflict of interest code is not a new concept.
feveral elected special districts have included candidate disclosure since 1977.,,
Considering this longstanding interpretation of the Act, new Sectio n g7302.3(a) would
serve little purpose unless it is more broadly interpreted to require SEIs from all
candidates for an elective office if the officl is designated in an agency,s conflict-of-
interest code.
lf you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (g16) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: Brian G. Lau
Counsel, Legal Division
BGLjgl
